title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
summary: Introduced GPT-3, a 175-billion parameter autoregressive language model, and demonstrated that scaling model size enables remarkable few-shot, one-shot, and zero-shot learning. By simply providing examples in the prompt (in-context learning), GPT-3 could perform tasks without any gradient updates or fine-tuning. This paper established the scaling paradigm and showed that model size alone could unlock emergent abilities.
key_contributions: Scaled language models to 175B parameters — 100x larger than GPT-2
Discovered in-context learning: task performance from examples in the prompt alone
Demonstrated emergent abilities in few-shot, one-shot, and zero-shot settings
Established scaling laws as the primary driver of language model capability
Showed that a single model could perform hundreds of NLP tasks without task-specific training
impact: The GPT-3 paper is one of the most influential AI papers ever published. It demonstrated that scale alone could produce remarkable generalization, established in-context learning as a new paradigm, and directly led to ChatGPT and the generative AI revolution. The paper shifted the entire field toward scaling up models and compute.
query_hooks: How does few-shot learning work in language models?
Why does scaling improve AI capabilities?
